Datura Stramonium, commonly known as Jimsonweed or Datura, is a toxic plant native to Europe and Asia, but naturalized in many parts of the world. The health benefits of Datura include its use as an antispasmodic and anxiolytic, although its toxic compounds can be hazardous in large doses. The bioactive constituents of Datura include alkaloids such as scopolamine and hyoscyamine, as well as solanine and solanidine. Herbal preparations made from Datura include tinctures, infusions, and ointments, which are typically used in homeopathic medicine to treat anxiety and insomnia, although their use should be approached with caution due to the plant's toxicity.
